dataset_inputs.n_band: The 369-firm estimate uses a 15.8% hospitals/healthcare-facilities margin proxy and employment-class averages; it is a floor rather than a census because some 20-99-employee dental groups may exceed $1 million of normalized EBITDA.

inputs.buy_mult / inputs.exit_mult: S8's 9x-11x band is for platform-grade regional groups; the deterministic size proxy establishes average EBITDA but not the platform attributes required for that band, so 10x remains directional.

inputs.owners_60plus_pct: The selected 42% is a judgmental interpolation rather than a source-reported statistic; a uniform split of HARDI's 55-64 band would imply about 38%, and the survey mixes owners/executives with other contractor respondents.
inputs.ai_replaceable_share: The three-digit 523 role mix includes brokerage and large asset-manager occupations and is low quality for exact NAICS 523940.
inputs.ai_replaceable_share: AI-use evidence does not directly measure headcount removal, and fiduciary/compliance review remains.
inputs.pi_dist: Fee structure varies sharply between wealth advice, institutional portfolio management, and private funds inside the code.
inputs.pi_moat: Regulatory duty supports accountable service but does not require a human-only delivery model and is not treated as a complete moat.
inputs.t50_years: Schwab participants are custody clients and any-use is broader than effective adoption; compliance restrictions can slow deployment.
inputs.current_adoption_pct: No public exact-NAICS measure of scaled AI workflow adoption or labor removal was found.
inputs.historical_analogs: Robo-adviser history predates conversational AI and may understate substitution by more capable systems.
inputs.owners_60plus_pct: Evidence is for advisers across channels, not owners of exact-code firms, and retirement horizon is not an age-60 cross-tab.
inputs.owners_60plus_pct.succession_shortage_documented: A transition challenge does not mean every retiring practice lacks a successor or will sell.
inputs.active_consolidators: Eight is a conservative documented subset, not the full buyer universe.
inputs.buy_mult: Median deal value is not a size-tier quote for every $1-10M EBITDA target; adjusted EBITDA, equity, earnouts, and retention payments can inflate headline comparability.
inputs.exit_mult: Exit premium is not directly observed for a defined $1-10M EBITDA cohort and should not be underwritten without quality improvement; market rates and structure may overwhelm it.
inputs.pricing_structure: Fee-offering incidence is not revenue-weighted and exact-code subsegments differ.
dataset_inputs.n_band: AUM bands are broad and log interpolation is an assumption, not an observed firm count.
dataset_inputs.n_band: The 0.88% fee is a client-account benchmark, not firmwide realized revenue/AUM, and the 29.9% margin is from 2019.
dataset_inputs.n_band: 523940 includes institutional asset managers and private-fund advisers whose fee and margin economics differ from wealth RIAs.
dataset_inputs.n_band: State-registered advisers are excluded from the selected count; a small number could nevertheless reach the EBITDA band.
inputs.ai_replaceable_share: The 52421 occupation mix includes MGAs, wholesale brokers and delegated-authority work as well as retail agencies; target books can differ materially.
inputs.ai_replaceable_share: Fractions describe technically replaceable tasks at mature deployment, not current adoption or immediate headcount reduction.
inputs.pi_dist: Carrier commission schedules and contingent income are controlled upstream and can change; fee-based large-commercial accounts expose cost more directly.
inputs.pi_moat: Commercial-heavy agencies have more moat than personal-lines and standardized small-commercial books; licensing is not treated as an exclusive channel franchise.
inputs.t50_years: The survey measures agency-reported usage rather than verified labor substitution, and target sophistication varies sharply by size.
inputs.current_adoption_pct: National Big I member survey; regular strategic use is a stricter definition than any AI use and does not prove headcount removal.
inputs.historical_analogs: E-signature is a simpler, lower-liability tool than AI-assisted policy advice or autonomous workflow execution.
inputs.owners_60plus_pct: More than 450 principals and owners were surveyed; it is not a Census estimate and may not match the $1–10M EBITDA cohort.
inputs.owners_60plus_pct.succession_shortage_documented: A documented channel-wide challenge does not mean every target lacks an internal successor.
inputs.active_consolidators: North American announced-deal data covers insurance distribution broadly and includes transactions outside exact NAICS 524210 or the target EBITDA band.
inputs.buy_mult: MarshBerry mixes roll-ins, stand-alone firms and platforms and skews larger than many $1–10M EBITDA targets; earnouts and contingent consideration are excluded.

inputs.exit_mult: Exit requires genuine platform scale, organic growth, retention and infrastructure; a collection of small books may still price as a roll-in, and 2026 public comparables had fallen toward 12x.
inputs.pricing_structure: The Hanover is one carrier example and does not establish a universal commission percentage.

dataset_inputs.n_band: The deterministic EBITDA-band count applies a 23.57% Damodaran Insurance (General) margin to insurance agencies and brokerages; although labeled ESTIMATE and transparently derived, this cross-segment margin proxy can move the buyability factor.
inputs.ai_replaceable_share: The 3-digit 531 role mix is not representative of exact 531210; maintenance, janitorial, rental and property-management roles largely belong to sibling codes.
inputs.ai_replaceable_share: Most real estate agents are independent contractors, so automation of agent tasks is not equivalent to reducing the supplied W-2 wage base or improving brokerage EBITDA.
inputs.pi_dist: Compass and Real are large public models, not a direct measure of every private brokerage; savings from automating 1099 agent work are not operator labor savings.
inputs.pi_moat: A dominant local brand with company-owned leads can be materially stronger; an owner-dependent or agent-portable book can be weaker.
inputs.t50_years: NAR measures individual REALTOR use, not verified brokerage process coverage or labor replacement.
inputs.current_adoption_pct: The survey had a 2.5% response rate and is member-level rather than firm-level; tool use is a broad proxy for effective adoption.
inputs.historical_analogs: Generative AI may unbundle advice and discovery more deeply than prior workflow tools, so the analog may understate terminal pressure.
inputs.owners_60plus_pct: NAR members include sales agents, associate brokers and nonowners; member age cannot be treated as direct owner age.
inputs.owners_60plus_pct.succession_shortage_documented: Firm-level succession planning must be diligenced; member demographics alone do not prove a channel-wide shortage.
inputs.active_consolidators: These are large strategic transactions, not proof of a deep, repeatable buyer pool for independent $1–10M EBITDA brokerages.
inputs.buy_mult: Axial's category may include business mixes beyond exact residential 531210; the single comp is anonymized, and EBITDA must be normalized after owner production and agent splits.

inputs.exit_mult: REMAX includes global franchise and mortgage assets, brand value and $30M expected synergies; it is not a clean independent-brokerage comp, and Axial's observed range is extremely wide.
inputs.pricing_structure: Owned brokerage, franchise, cap, desk-fee, team and ancillary-service models have different revenue bases; compare valuations only on normalized retained EBITDA.

Compass's filing reports consolidated 2025 commissions and related expense of $5,679.7 million on $6,961.6 million revenue (81.6%), not 81.1% of early-2026 stand-alone brokerage revenue; this correction does not change the low-capture conclusion.
